title: HomeCraft Black Stainless Steel Full-Size Kegerator Draft Beer Dispenser & Beer Cooler, Spring-Loaded Tap Dispenser, Holds 1/6, 1/4, 1/2 Barrels, 2.5-Pound CO2 Tank, Single Meter Regulator

cooling_failure: I was really liking the Kegerator, after using it with a variety of refillable growlers and gravity feed aluminum 5L kegs. Unfortunately 6 months into my ownership the cooling system has failed and will no longer keep my beverage below room temperature.

I received this 3 days ago and followed the instructions on chilling the beer keg for at least 10 hours, before placing it in the system. It’s been over 2 days and the machine will not cool down past 58 degrees.
It says this is not eligible for return, but I bought the warranty, which I suppose is rendered useless ?
control_failure: I bought this as a gift for my wife who wanted to have her favorite beer on tap at home.  This is legitimately a gimmick.  Its impossible to setup and operate correctly.  It uses C02 cartridges that literally run out within a glass or two.  The flow is impossible to control, its hard to tell if you pressurized the kegurator, and the temp has trouble staying where its supposed to be.  It took us 6
physical_breakage: On the 2nd use, the plastic dip tube got stuck (it's a rubber fitting to my keg), and in trying to pull it out, the plastic threads on the fitting have stripped and are not reusable. Replacement parts available? I doubt it. These parts really need to be made out of metal so they won't break. Delivered Aug 21st, this is Sept 16th. Less than a month. I am a homebrewer and I own one of these I purchased from HomeDepot. They're really great if you're a small-batch brewer (I was bring 3gal batches at the time which means two 1.2gal mini-kegs were perfect for my useable beer once separated from the trub), or if you have a source for regularly purchasing Heineken-style minikegs of ready made beer (by the way, Hofbrauhaus ships their Oktoberfestbier
leak:
[[VIDEOID:d1629a029da8b8099849a699722762b5]] After one use the tap won't stop leaking. It's leaking not only through the nozzle but around the outside as well.

I've been brewing beer for a decade now. I have a dual tap kegerator I built myself that works flawlessly. I had hoped I could use this in my tiki bar as a portable mini kegerator but it's just not working. At all. The tap i

Everything seemed fine… into our third keg… figuring out good C02 combo for the perfect pour … and after having it for 2.5 months this black liquid starts leaking.  What’s up with this? Still holding temp so far… but what is it? Motor oil? Is motor oil in the compressor?  Seriously less than three months ownership. Window of return was minimal… now what?
stopped_working: I had this product for a little under 3 weeks and it stoped working for no reason, i was looking forward to having it for the long weekend with some friends

poured a couple beers (less than a 6-pack). Never worked again.

Tried to fix by trying Growler method but by time I could procure another keg (they NOT easy to find), 30-day return up.
